title: document obtaining images from local dockerd
body:
I had a brief irc conversation with @nalind back then:

```
ttomecek>   nalind, hi, I'm trying buildah: I built a custom docker using
    docker and I want to use this image in buildah, so I'm running `sudo buildah
    from fwp` and it fails with docker registry error
ttomecek>   denied: requested access to the resource is denied
ttomecek>   unauthorized: authentication required
ttomecek>   is it possible to disable pull somehow? (so it just copies the
    image from local docker daemon?)
nalind> ttomecek: it doesn't share local storage with dockerd, so to pull an
    image from the local daemon, you could refer to the image, at least the
    first time, as 'docker-daemon:fwp:latest' and let it grab a copy
```

I am trying this out:

```
$ buildah from --pull=false docker-daemon:fedora:26-with-python
ERRO[0000] error parsing reference to image "docker-daemon:fedora:26-with-python": invalid reference format

$ docker images fedora                                                                                                                          1 ↵
REPOSITORY          TAG                 IMAGE ID            CREATED             SIZE
fedora              26-with-python      57603fc684b5        10 days ago         409 MB
docker.io/fedora    26                  ac96429054d6        2 weeks ago         231 MB
fedora              25-with-python      0881717adf14        2 weeks ago         447 MB
docker.io/fedora    25                  15895ef0b3b2        3 months ago        231 MB
```

Can you help me out here?

comments.body: @TomasTomecek What happens if you remove pull=false?

doesn't work :/

```
$ buildah from docker-daemon://fedora:26-with-python
ERRO[0000] error parsing reference to image "docker-daemon://fedora:26-with-python": invalid reference format
$ buildah from docker-daemon:fedora:26-with-python
ERRO[0000] error parsing reference to image "docker-daemon:fedora:26-with-python": invalid reference format
$ buildah from docker-daemon:fedora:26
ERRO[0000] error parsing reference to image "docker-daemon:fedora:26": invalid reference format
```

```
$ rpm -q buildah
buildah-0.3-2.gitb9b2a8a.fc27.x86_64
```

Does skopeo copy work

skopeo copy docker-daemon:fedora:26-with-python dir:/tmp/fedora

yup, that works just fine:

```
$ skopeo copy docker-daemon:fedora:26-with-python dir:/root/test
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:762cbde810b0293a01c82daca36ba7f3360f821fdd658809e8363309836d3b7f
 228.87 MB / 228.87 MB [=======================================================]
Copying blob sha256:1fbb7a5cc3f834a37ef2f9f219bc7a100448e1f56fb6817760f5f29d0a869698
 172.14 MB / 172.14 MB [=======================================================]
Copying config sha256:57603fc684b5cb6b0302470f1cc6ab3958dd9fdd2eaa94ca062f9186469212f7
 0 B / 2.30 KB [---------------------------------------------------------------]
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
```

From an uninformed code inspection, this could be https://github.com/projectatomic/buildah/blob/33c2a8a418579db3e59a66bb925eaf627fa74623/pull.go#L18 + https://github.com/projectatomic/buildah/blob/master/cmd/buildah/from.go#L13 .

Try perhaps something like
```sh
buildah from --registry docker-daemon: fedora:26-with-python
```
(including the space after `docker-daemon:`).

(BTW that `--registry` option seems rather misnamed.)

> (BTW that --registry option seems rather misnamed.)

I have the same feeling, see #108

I have added 
https://github.com/projectatomic/buildah/pull/234
to remove the registry flag.
What is the status here? I got lost in the pull requests.
@TomSweeneyRedHat Could you check if this works now?

Doesn't work now, in fact I'm not able to get skopeo to work either:

```
[root@localhost buildah]# docker images
REPOSITORY          TAG                 IMAGE ID            CREATED             SIZE
docker.io/fedora    latest              b0b140824a48        12 days ago         231.1 MB

[root@localhost buildah]# buildah from docker-daemon://docker.io/fedora:latest
ERRO[0000] error parsing image name "docker://docker-daemon://docker.io/fedora:latest": invalid reference format 

[root@localhost buildah]# skopeo copy docker-deamon:docker.io/fedora:latest dir:/root/test
FATA[0000] Invalid source name docker-deamon:docker.io/fedora:latest: Invalid image name "docker-deamon:docker.io/fedora:latest", unknown transport "docker-deamon" 

```

I'll take a look at the buildah error, that's looking like a recent change I made to add the "docker://' transport might be a little over-reactive.

```console
[root@localhost buildah]# buildah from docker-daemon://docker.io/fedora:latest
ERRO[0000] error parsing image name "docker://docker-daemon://docker.io/fedora:latest": invalid reference format 
```
`docker-daemon:` syntax does not use `//` (the only one which does is `docker://`)

```console
[root@localhost buildah]# skopeo copy docker-deamon:docker.io/fedora:latest dir:/root/test
FATA[0000] Invalid source name docker-deamon:docker.io/fedora:latest: Invalid image name "docker-deamon:docker.io/fedora:latest", unknown transport "docker-deamon" 
```
This is just a typo, d**ea**mon vs d**ae**mon.

`skopeo copy` with the typo fixed works for me; I didn’t try `buildah from`.

@mtrmac Thanks for the eagle eye, my fingers wiggled too quickly on the skopeo command.  It does indeed work for me now with the corrected spelling for daemon.  I'll take a look at from, I'm not sure what's going on there.

I was testing with Tomas' second example and that's still failing, but I think it's failing as it's bad input.  If I use the format in his first example which removes the two slashes after 'docker-daemon:' it's working.

```
[root@localhost buildah]# buildah from  docker-daemon:docker.io/fedora:latest
fedora-working-container-3
```

I say we can close this one unless we want to give Tomas a chance to test it.  @mtrmac thanks again for the heads up on the typo.
@TomasTomecek Repoen if you don't agree with the analysys.  We should do a better job of diagnosing typos though.
